evals.name: blank-page-draft
evals.prompt: make me a resume. i'm a backend engineer, about 5 years, worked at Stripe and a startup before that. targeting senior backend roles.
evals.expected_output: Skill drafts a complete first-draft PDF immediately (inventing plausible content marked with the ‹?› token), asks for/acknowledges the target role, offers template previews, then interviews to replace guesses with real facts, reviews the collected info, and builds a final clean PDF with no ‹?› markers left.
expectations: A real PDF was produced (build ok:true, file exists).
A complete first-draft resume was built EARLY (draft-first, before the interview finished).
The early draft invented plausible content marked with the ‹?› token.
The skill offered a template choice (previews/named options, set meta.template).
The skill interviewed the user rather than fabricating everything silently.
The final resume has no remaining ‹?› markers.
The skill did NOT open with a long questionnaire before drafting.

evals.name: ingest-and-tailor
evals.prompt: here's my current resume (a .txt file) — can you rework it for this Senior Platform Engineer role at Northwind Cloud? job posting is in the provided file too.
evals.expected_output: Skill ingests the local old-resume file and the job-posting file, tailors the resume to the posting (reorders/surfaces k8s + reliability + SLO + tooling experience, mirrors real keywords, no fabrication), coaches the vague bullets into action+impact+number form, and builds a tailored PDF.

evals.name: coach-weak-bullets
evals.prompt: i already started my resume but the bullets feel weak and generic. can you make them stronger? (provides 4 flat duty-list bullets for a data engineer)
evals.expected_output: Skill rewrites each weak duty-list bullet into action-verb + specific-outcome + quantified-impact form, asking the user for the missing numbers, and produces a stronger set of bullets (and builds a PDF).
